author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm CII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, one poor man prayeth, and prayeth not in silence.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We may therefore hear him, and see who he is: whether it be not perchance He, of whom the Apostle saith, “Though He was rich, yet for your sakes He became poor, that ye through His poverty might be rich.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If it is He, then, how is He poor?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in what sense He is rich, who seeth not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then is richer than He, by whom riches were made, even those which are not true riches?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For through Him we have even these riches, ability, memory, character, health of body, the senses, and the conformation of our limbs: for when these are safe, even the poor are rich.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Through Him also are those greater riches, faith, piety, justice, charity, chastity, good conduct: for no man hath these, except through Him who justifieth the ungodly.…Behold, how rich!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In one so rich, how are we to recognise these words?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have eaten ashes as it were bread: and mingled my drink with weeping.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Have these so great riches come to this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The former state is a very high one, this is a very lowly one.…Yet still examine whether this poor man be He; since, “The Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Reflect also upon these words: “I am Thy servant, and the Son of Thine handmaid.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Observe, this handmaid, chaste, a virgin, and a mother: for there He received our poverty, when He was clothed in the form of a servant, emptying Himself; lest thou shouldest dread His riches, and in thy beggarly state shouldest not dare approach Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There, I say, He put on the form of a servant, there He was clothed with our poverty; there He made Himself poor, and us rich.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We are now drawing near to understand these things of Him: nevertheless we may not as yet rashly pronounce.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let him add poverty then to poverty: let Him transfigure unto Himself our humble body: let Him be our Head, we His limbs, let there be two in one flesh.…For He hath deigned to hold even us as His limbs.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The penitent also are among His limbs.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they are not shut out, nor separated from His Church: nor would He make the Church His spouse, unless by words like these: “Repent ye, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us then hear what the head and the body prayeth, the bridegroom and bride, Christ and the Church, both one Person; but the Word and the flesh are not both one thing; the Father and the Word are both one thing; Christ and the Church are both one Person, one perfect man in the form of His own fulness.…Let us hear therefore Christ, poor within us and with us, and for our sakes.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the title itself indicates the poor one.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lastly, remember that I conjectured who that poor one was: let us hear His prayer, and recognise His Person; and mistake not, when thou shalt have heard anything that cannot apply to His Head; it was for this reason that I have prefaced as I have, that whatever thou shalt hear of this description, thou mayest understand as sounding from the weakness of the body, and recognise the voice of the members in the head.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The title is, “A Prayer of the afflicted, when he was tormented, and poured out his prayer before the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is the same poor one who elsewhere saith: “From the ends of the earth will I call upon Thee, when my heart is in heaviness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is afflicted because He is also Christ; who in the Prophet’s words calleth Himself both Bridegroom and Bride: “He hath bound on me the diadem as on a bridegroom, and as a bride hath adorned me with an ornament.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He called Himself Bridegroom, He called Himself Bride; wherefore this, unless Bridegroom applieth to the Head, Bride to the body?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They are one voice then, because they are one flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us hear, and recognise ourselves in these words; and if we see that we are without, let us labour to be there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Hear my prayer, O Lord: and let my crying come unto Thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Hear my prayer, O Lord,” is the same as, “Let my crying come unto Thee:” the feeling of the suppliant is shown by the repetition.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Turn not Thy face away from me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When did God turn away His Face from His Son? when did the Father turn away His Face from Christ?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But for the sake of the poverty of my members, “Turn not away Thy face from me: whatsoever day I am troubled, incline Thine ear unto me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 2).…Thou art in trouble this day, I am in trouble; another is in trouble to-morrow, I am in trouble; after this generation other descendants, who succeed your descendants, are in trouble, I am in trouble; down to the end of the world, whoever are in trouble in My body, I am in trouble.…Peter prayed, Paul prayed, the rest of the Apostles prayed; the faithful prayed in those times, the faithful prayed in the following times, the faithful prayed in the times of the Martyrs, the faithful pray in our times, the faithful will pray in the times of our descendants.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Right soon:” for I now ask that which Thou art willing to grant.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I ask not earthly things, as an earthly man; but redeemed at last from my former captivity, I long for the kingdom of heaven; “Hear me right soon:” for it is only to such a longing that Thou hast said, “Even while Thou art speaking, I will say, Here I am.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore dost thou call? in what tribulation? in what want?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O poor one, before the gate of God all-rich, in what longing dost thou beg? from what destitution dost thou ask relief? from what want dost thou knock, that it may be opened unto thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For my days are consumed away like smoke” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O days! if days: for where day is heard of, light is understood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My days,” my times; wherefore, “like smoke,” unless from the puffing up of pride?…See smoke, like pride, ascending, swelling, vanishing: deservedly therefore failing, and not stedfast.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And my bones are scorched up as it were in an oven.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Both my bones, and my strength, not without tribulation, not without burning.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The bones of the body of Christ, the strength of His body, is it anywhere greater than in the Holy Apostles?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And yet see that the bones are scorched.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who is offended, and I burn not?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They are brave, faithful, able interpreters and preachers of the word, living as they speak, speaking as they hear; they are clearly brave, yet all who suffer offences, are an oven to them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there is love there, and more so in the bones.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The bones are within all the flesh, and support all the flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if any man suffer any offence, and endanger his soul; the bone is scorched in proportion as it loveth.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Look back to Adam, whence the human race sprung.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For how but from him was misery propagated? whence but from him is this hereditary poverty?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let him then, who in his own body was at one time in despair, now that he is set in Christ’s body, say with hope, “My heart is smitten down, and withered like grass” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Deservedly, since all flesh is grass.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But how did this happen unto thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Since I have forgotten to eat my bread.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For God had given His commandment for bread.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what is the bread of the soul?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The serpent suggesting, and the woman transgressing, he touched the forbidden fruit, he forgot the commandment: his heart was smitten as it deserved, and withered like grass, since he forgot to eat his bread.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Having forgotten to eat bread, he drinketh poison: his heart is smitten, and withered like grass.…Now eat that bread which thou hadst forgotten.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But this very Bread hath come, in whose body thou mayest remember the voice of thy forgetfulness, and cry out in thy poverty, so that thou mayest receive riches.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now eat: for thou art in His body, who saith, “I am the living bread which came down from heaven.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hadst forgotten to eat thy bread; but after His crucifixion, “all the ends of the earth shall be reminded, and be converted unto the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: After forgetfulness, let remembrance come, let bread be eaten from heaven, that we may live; not manna, as they did eat, and died; that bread, of which it is said, “Blessed are they who hunger and thirst after righteousness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the voice of my groaning, the bones cleave unto my flesh” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For many groan, and I also groan; even for this I groan, because they groan for a wrong cause.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That man hath lost a piece of money, he groaneth: he hath lost faith, he groaneth not: I weigh the money and the faith, and I find more cause for groaning for him who groaneth not as he ought, or doth not groan at all.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He committeth fraud, and rejoiceth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With what gain, with what loss?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath gained money, he hath lost righteousness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the latter reason, he who knoweth how to groan, groaneth; he who is near the head, who righteously clingeth to Christ’s body, groaneth for this reason.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the carnal do not groan for this reason, and they cause themselves to be groaned for, because they do not groan for this reason; nor can we despise them, whether they groan not at all, or groan for the wrong cause.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we wish to correct them, we wish to amend them, we wish to reform them, and when we cannot, we groan; and when we groan, we are not separated from them.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I am become like a pelican in the wilderness, and like an owl among ruined walls” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold three birds and three places: the pelican, the owl, and the sparrow; and the three places are severally, the wilderness, the ruined walls, and the house-top.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The pelican in the wilderness, the owl in the ruined walls, and the sparrow in the house-top.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the first place we must explain, what the pelican signifieth: since it is born in a region which maketh it unknown to us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is born in lonely spots, especially those of the river Nile in Egypt.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whatever kind of bird it is, let us consider what the Psalm intended to say of it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “It dwelleth,” it saith, “in the wilderness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why enquire of its form, its limbs, its voice, its habits?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As far as the Psalm telleth thee, it is a bird that dwelleth in solitude.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The owl is a bird that loveth night.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Parietinæ, or ruins, as we call them, are walls standing without roof, without inhabitants, these are the habitation of the owl.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And then as to the house-top and the sparrows, ye are familiar with them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I find, therefore, some one of Christ’s body, a preacher of the word, sympathizing with the weak, seeking the gains of Christ, mindful of his Lord to come.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us see these three things from the office of His steward.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hath such a man come among those who are not Christians?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is a pelican in the wilderness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hath he come among those who were Christians, and have relapsed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is an owl in the ruined walls; for he forsaketh not even the darkness of those who dwell in night, he wisheth to gain even these.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hath he come among such as are Christians dwelling in a house, not as if they believed not, or as if they had let go what they had believed, but walking lukewarmly in what they believe?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The sparrow crieth unto them, not in the wilderness, because they are Christians; nor in the ruined walls, because they have not relapsed; but because they are within the roof; under the roof rather, because they are under the flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The sparrow above the flesh crieth out, husheth not up the commandments of God, nor becometh carnal, so that he be subject to the roof.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “What ye hear in the ear, that preach ye on the housetops.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There are three birds and three places; and one man may represent the three birds, and three men may represent severally the three birds; and the three sorts of places, are three classes of men: yet the wilderness, the ruined walls, and the house-top, are but three classes of men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 8. …Let us not pass over what is said, or even read, of this bird, that is, the pelican; not rashly asserting anything, but yet not passing over what has been left to be read and uttered by those who have written it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do ye so hear, that if it be true, it may agree; if false, it may not hold.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These birds are said to slay their young with blows of their beaks, and for three days to mourn them when slain by themselves in the nest: after which they say the mother wounds herself deeply, and pours forth her blood over her young, bathed in which they recover life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This may be true, it may be false: yet if it be true, see how it agreeth with Him, who gave us life by His blood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It agreeth with Him in that the mother’s flesh recalleth to life her young with her blood; it agreeth well.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He calleth Himself a hen brooding over her young.…If, then, it be so truly, this bird doth closely resemble the flesh of Christ, by whose blood we have been called to life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But how may it agree with Christ, that the bird herself slays her own young?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Doth not this agree with it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will slay, and I will make alive: I will wound, and I will heal.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Would the persecutor Saul have died, unless he were wounded from heaven; or would the preacher be raised up, unless by life given him from His blood?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But let those who have written on the subject see to this; we ought not to allow our understanding of it to rest upon doubtful ground.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us rather recognise this bird in the wilderness; as the Psalm expresseth it, “A pelican in the solitude.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I suppose that Christ born of a Virgin is here meant.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was born in loneliness, because He alone was thus born.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: After the nativity, we come to His Passion.…Born in the wilderness, because alone so born; suffering in the darkness of the Jews as it were in night, in their sin, as it were in ruins: what next?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have watched:” and “am become even as it were a sparrow, that sitteth alone upon the house-top” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hadst then slept amid the ruins, and hadst said, “I laid me down, and slept.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meaneth, “I slept”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because I chose, I slept: I slept for love of night: but, “I rose again,” followeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore “I watched,” is here said.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But after He watched, what did He?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He ascended into heaven, He became as a sparrow by flying; that is, by ascending; “alone on the house-top;” that is, in heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is therefore as the pelican by birth, as the owl by dying, as the sparrow by ascending again: there in the wilderness, as one alone; here in the ruined walls, as one slain by those who could not stand in the building; and here again watching and flying for our sakes alone on the house-top, He there intercedeth in our behalf.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For our Head is as the sparrow, His body as the turtle-dove.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the sparrow hath found her an house.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In heaven, where He doth mediate for us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the turtle-dove a nest,” the Church of God hath found a nest from the wood of His Cross, where “she may lay her young,” her children.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Mine enemies revile me all day, and they that praised me are sworn together against me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With their mouth they praised, in their heart they were laying snares for me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear their praise: “Master, we know that Thou art true, and teachest the way of God in truth, neither carest Thou for any man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it lawful to give tribute unto Cæsar, or not?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And whence this evil repute, except because I came to make sinners my members, that by repentance they may be in my body?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thence is all the calumny, thence the persecution.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Why eateth your Master with publicans and sinners?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They that be whole need not a physician, but they that be sick.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Would that ye were aware of your sickness, that ye might seek a physician; ye would not slay Him, and through your infatuated pride perish in a false health.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have eaten ashes as it were bread: and mingled my drink with weeping” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because He chose to have among His members these kinds of men, that they should be healed and set free, thence is the evil repute.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now at this day what is the character of Pagan calumny against us? what, brethren, do ye conceive they tell us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye corrupt discipline, and pervert the morality of the human race.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why dost thou attack us; say why? what have we done?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By giving, he replieth, to men room for repentance, by promising impunity for all sins: for this reason men do evil deeds, careless of consequences, because everything is pardoned them, when they are converted.…And what is to become of thee, miserable man, if there shall be no harbour of impunity?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If there is only licence for sinning, and no pardon for sins, where wilt thou be, whither wilt thou go?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Surely even for thee did it happen, that that afflicted one ate ashes as it were bread, and mingled His drink with weeping.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Doth not such a feast now please thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But nevertheless, he replieth, men add to their sins under the hope of pardon.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nay, but they would add to them if they despaired of pardon.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou not observe in what licentious cruelty gladiators live? whence this, except because, as destined for the sword and sacrifice, they choose to sate their lust, before they pour forth their blood?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wouldest not thou also thus address thyself?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I am already a sinner, already an unjust man, one already doomed to damnation, hope of pardon there is none: why should I not do whatever pleaseth me, although it be not lawful? why not fulfil, as far as I can, any longings I may have, if, after these, nothing but torments only be in store?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wouldest thou not thus speak unto thyself, and from this very despair become still worse?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Rather than this, then, He who promiseth forgiveness, doth correct thee, saying, “As I live, saith the Lord, I have no pleasure in the death of the wicked; but that the wicked turn from his way and live.”…For in order that men might not live the worse from despair, He promised a harbour of forgiveness; again, that they might not live the worse from hope of pardon, He made the day of death uncertain: fixing both with the utmost providence, both as a refuge for the returning, and a terror to the loitering.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Eat ashes as bread, and mingle thy drink with weeping; by means of this banquet thou shalt reach the table of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Despair not; pardon hath been promised thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thanks be to God, he saith, because it is promised; I hold fast the promise of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now therefore live well.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To-morrow, he replieth, I will live well.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God hath promised the pardon; no one promised thee to-morrow.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And that because of thine indignation and wrath: because thou hast taken me up, thou hast cast me down” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is thy wrath, O Lord, in Adam: that wrath in which we were all born, which cleaveth unto us by our birth; the wrath froth the stock of iniquity, the wrath from the mass of sin: according to what the Apostle saith, “We also were once the children of wrath, even as others.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He saith not, the wrath of God shall come upon him: but, “abideth upon him:” because that wrath in which he was born is not taken away.…Man set in honour, is made in the image of God: raised up to this honour, lifted up from the dust, from the earth, he hath received a reasonable soul; by the vivacity of that very reason, he is placed before all beasts, cattle, birds that fly, and fishes.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For which of these hath reason to understand?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because none of them is created in the image of God.…Therefore, “Because Thou hast taken me up, Thou hast cast me down:” punishment followeth me, because Thou hast given me a free choice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if Thou hadst not given me a free choice, and for this reason didst not make me better than cattle, just condemnation would not follow me when I sinned.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus Thou hast taken me up in giving me freedom of choice, and by Thy judgment Thou hast cast me down.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My days have declined like a shadow” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 11).…He had said above, “My days are consumed away like smoke;” and he now saith, “My days have declined like a shadow.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In this shadow, day must be recognised; in this shadow, light must be discerned; lest afterward it be said in late and fruitless repentance, “What hath pride profited us? or what good hath riches with our vaunting brought us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All those things are passed away like a shadow.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Say at this season, all things will pass away like a shadow, and thou mayest not pass away like a shadow.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My days have declined like a shadow, and I am withered like grass.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he had said above, “my heart is smitten down, and I am withered like grass.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the grass bedewed with the Saviour’s blood will flourish afresh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have withered like grass;” I, that is, man, after that disobedience; this I have suffered from Thy just judgment: but what art Thou?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For not because I have fallen, hast Thou grown old: for Thou art strong to set me free, who hast been strong to humble me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But Thou, O Lord, endurest for ever: and Thy remembrance throughout all generations” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thy remembrance,” because Thou dost not forget: “throughout all generations,” forasmuch as we know the promise of life, both present and future.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou shalt arise, and have mercy upon Sion: for it is time that Thou have mercy upon her” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But when the fulness of time was come, God sent forth His Son, made of a woman, made under the Law.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And where is Sion?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “To redeem them that were under the Law.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: First then were the Jews: for thence were the Apostles, thence those more than five hundred brethren, thence that later multitude, who had but one heart and one soul toward God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, “the time is come.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Behold, now is the accepted time: behold, now is the day of salvation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who saith this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That Servant of God, that Builder, who said, “Ye are God’s building.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here therefore what saith he?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For thy servants take pleasure in her stones” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In whose stones?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the stones of Sion?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But there are those there that are not stones.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not stones of what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then followeth? “and pity the dust thereof.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I understand by the stones of Sion all the Prophets: there was the voice of preaching sent before, thence the ministry of the Gospel assumed, through their preaching Christ became known.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore thy servants have taken pleasure in the stones of Sion.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But those faithless apostates from God, who offended their Creator by their evil deeds, have returned to the earth, whence they were taken.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have become dust, they have become ungodly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But wait, Lord; bear with us, Lord; be long-suffering, O Lord: let not the wind rush in, and sweep away this dust from the face of the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let thy servants come, let them come, let them acknowledge in the stones thy voice, let them pity the dust of Sion, let them be formed in thy image: let the dust say, lest it perish, “Remember that we are but dust.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This of Sion: was not that which crucified the Lord, dust?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is worse, it was dust from the ruined walls; altogether dust it was, but nevertheless it was not in vain said of this dust, “Father, forgive them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From this very dust there came a wall of so many thousands who believed, and who laid the price of their possessions at the Apostles’ feet.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From that dust then there arose a human nature formed and beautiful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who among the heathen acted thus?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How few are there whom we admire for having done thus, compared with the many thousands of these converts?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: At first suddenly three, afterwards five thousand; all living in unity, all laying the price of their possessions, when they had sold them, at the Apostles’ feet, that it might be distributed to each, as each had need, who had one soul and one heart toward God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who made this even of that very dust, but He who created Adam himself out of dust?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This then is concerning Sion, but not in Sion only.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The heathen shall fear Thy Name, O Lord; and all the kings of the earth Thy Majesty” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now that Thou hast pitied Sion, now that Thy servants have taken pleasure in her stones, by acknowledging the foundation of the Apostles and Prophets; now that they have pitied her dust; so that man is formed, or rather re-formed, in life out of dust; hence preaching hath increased among the heathen: let the heathen fear Thy Name, let another wall approach also from the heathen, let the Corner Stone be recognised, let the two who come from different regions, but who no longer differ in belief, meet in close union.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the Lord shall build up Sion” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This work is going on now.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O ye living stones, run to the work of building, not to ruin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sion is in building, beware of the ruined walls: the tower is building, the ark is in building; remember the deluge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This work is in progress now; but when Sion is built, what will happen?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And He will appear in His glory.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That He might build up Sion, that He might be a foundation in Sion, He was seen by Sion, but not in His glory: “we have seen Him, and He had no form nor comeliness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But truly when He shall have come with His angels to judge, shall they not look then upon Him whom they have pierced? and they shall be put to confusion when too late, who refused confusion in early and healthful repentance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He hath turned Him unto the prayer of the poor destitute, and despised not their desire” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is going on now in the building of Sion: the builders of Sion pray, they groan: He is the one poor, because the poor are many; because the thousands among so many nations are one in Him, because He is the unity of the peace of the Church, He is one, He is many: one, through love: many, on account of His extension.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore we now pray, we now run: now, if any man hath used to be otherwise, and lived differently, let him eat ashes as it were bread, and mingle his drink with weeping.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now is the time, when Sion is in building: now the stones are entering into the structure: when the building is finished, and the house dedicated, why dost thou run, to ask when too late, to beg in vain, to knock to no purpose, doomed to abide without with the five foolish virgins?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore now run.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let these things be written for those that come after” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When these words were written, they profited not so much those among whom they were written for they were written to prophesy the New Testament, among men who lived according to the Old Testament.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But God had both given that Old Testament, and had settled in that land of promise His own people.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But since “Thy remembrance is from generation to generation,” belongeth not to the ungodly, but to the righteous; “in our generation” belongeth to the Old Testament; while “in the other generation” belongeth to the New Testament; and since the New Testament announceth this that was prophesied, “Let these things be written for those that come after: and the people which shall be created, shall praise the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not the people which is created, but “the people which shall be created.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is clearer, my brethren?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here is prophesied that creation of which the Apostle saith: “Therefore if any man be in Christ, he is a new creature; old things are passed away; behold, all things are become new.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For he hath looked down from His lofty sanctuary.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath looked down from on high, that He might come unto the humble: from on high He hath become humble, that He might exalt the humble.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Out of the heaven did the Lord look down upon the earth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 19): “that He might hear the mournings of such as are in fetters, and deliver the children of such as are put to death” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We have found it said in another Psalm, “O let the sorrowful sighs of the fettered come before Thee;” and in a passage where the voice of the martyrs was meant.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence are the martyrs in fetters?…But God had bound them with these fetters, hard indeed and painful for a season, but endurable on account of His promises, unto whom it is said, “On account of the words of Thy lips, I have kept hard ways.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We must indeed groan in these fetters in order to gain the mercy of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These fetters must not be shunned, in order to gain a destructive freedom and the temporal and brief pleasure of this life, to be followed by perpetual bitterness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Accordingly Scripture, that we may not refuse the fetters of wisdom, thus addresseth us: “…Then shall her fetters be a strong defence for thee, and her chains a robe of glory.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the fettered therefore cry out, as long as they are in the chains of the discipline of God, in which the martyrs have been tried: the fetters shall be loosed, and they shall fly away, and these very fetters shall afterwards be turned into an ornament.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This hath happened with the martyrs.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what have the persecutors effected by killing them, except that their fetters were thereby loosed, and turned into crowns?…The remission of sins, is the loosing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what would it have profited Lazarus, that he came forth from the tomb, unless it were said to him, “loose him, and let him go”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Himself indeed with His voice aroused him from the tomb, Himself restored his life by crying unto him, Himself overcame the mass of earth that was heaped upon the tomb, and he came forth bound hand and foot: not therefore with his own feet, but by the power of Him who drew him forth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This taketh place in the heart of the penitent: when thou hearest a man is sorry for his sins, he hath already come again to life; when thou hearest him by confessing lay bare his conscience, he is already drawn forth from the tomb, but he is not as yet loosed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When is he loosed, and by whom is he loosed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Whatsoever thou shalt loose on earth,” He saith, “shall be loosed in Heaven.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Forgiveness of sins may justly be granted by the Church: but the dead man himself cannot be aroused except by the Lord crying within him; for God doth this within him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We speak to your ears: how do we know what may be going on in your hearts?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what is going on within, is not our doing, but His.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “That the name of the Lord may be declared in Sion” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For at first, when the fettered were appointed unto death, the Church was oppressed: since these tribulations the Name of the Lord has been declared in Sion, with great freedom, in the Church herself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For she is Sion: not that one spot, at first proud, afterwards taken captive; but the Sion whose shadow was that Sion, which signifieth a watch-tower; because when placed in the flesh, we see into the things before us, extending ourselves not to the present which is now, but to the future.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus it is a watch-tower: for every watcher gazes far.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Places where guards are set, are termed watch-towers: these are set on rocks, on mountains, in trees, that a wider prospect may be commanded from a higher eminence.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sion therefore is a watch-tower, the Church is a watch-tower.…If therefore the Church be a watch-tower, the Name of the Lord is already declared there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not the Lord’s Name only is declared in that Sion, but “His praise,” He saith, “in Jerusalem.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And how is it declared?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In the nations gathering together in one, and the kingdoms, that they may serve the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How is this accomplished, unless by the blood of the slain?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How accomplished, but by the groans of the fettered?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those therefore who were in tribulation and humility have been heard; that in our times the Church might be in the great glory which we see her in, so that the very kingdoms which then persecuted her, now serve the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “She answered Him in the way of His strength” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 23).…The preceding words show, that either “His praise,” or “Jerusalem,” answered: for it was said, “And His praise in Jerusalem; in the nations gathering together in one, and the kingdoms, that they may serve the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Respondit ei.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We cannot say, “the kingdoms answered,” for he would have said responderunt.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Respondit ei.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We cannot say, “the nations answered,” for he would have said, responderunt (in the plural).

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since then it is Respondit ei, in the singular, we look for the singular number above, and find that the words, “His praise,” and “Jerusalem,” are the only words in which we find it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But since it is doubtful, whether it be “His praise,” or “Jerusalem,” let us expound it each way.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How did “His praise” answer Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When they who are called by Him thank Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He calleth, we answer; not by our voice, but by our faith; not by our tongue, but by our life.…From His elect and holy men, Jerusalem also answereth Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Jerusalem also was called: and the first Jerusalem refused to hear, and it was said unto her, “Behold, thy house shall be left unto the desolate.”…But that Jerusalem, of whom it was written, “Sing, O barren, thou that didst not bear,” “She hath answered Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meaneth, “She hath answered Him”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: She despiseth Him not when He called.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He sent rain, She gave fruit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “She answered Him:” but where? “in the path of His strength.”…The Church therefore answered Him not in the way of weakness; because after His resurrection He called the Church from the whole world, no longer weak upon the cross, but strong in heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is not the praise of the Christian faith that they believe that Christ died, but that they believe that He arose from the dead.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even the Pagan believeth that He died; and maketh this a charge against thee, that thou hast believed in one dead.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then is thy praise?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is that thou believest that Christ arose from the dead, and that thou dost hope that thou shalt rise from the dead through Christ: this is the praise of faith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For if thou shalt confess with thy mouth that Jesus is the Lord, and shalt believe in thy heart that God hath raised Him from the dead, thou shalt be saved.”…This is the faith of Christians.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In this faith then, in which the Church is gathered, “She hath answered Him,” She gave Him worship according to His commandments: “in the path of His strength,” not in the path of His weakness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How she answered Him, ye have already heard above.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In the gathering of the nations into one.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Herein she answered Him, in unity: he who is not in unity, answereth Him not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He is One, the Church is unity: none but unity answereth to Him who is One.…Since some were destined to say against her, She hath existed, and no longer doth exist; “Show me,” He saith, “the shortness of my days,” what is it, that I know not what apostates from me murmur against me? why is it that lost men contend that I have perished?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they surely say this, that I have been, and no longer am: “Show me the shortness of my days.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I do not ask from Thee about those everlasting days: they are without end, where I shall be; it is not those I ask of: I ask of temporal days; show unto me my temporal days; “show me the shortness,” not the eternity, “of my days.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Declare unto me, how long I shall be in this world: on account of those who say, “She hath been,” and is no more: on account of those who say, The Scriptures are fulfilled, all nations have believed, but the Church hath become apostate, and hath perished from among all nations.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Seest thou not that there are still nations among whom the Gospel hath not been preached?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since then it is needful that what the Lord spoke shall be fulfilled, declaring unto the Church the shortness of my days, that this Gospel be preached in all nations, and then that the end may come, why is it that thou sayest that the Church hath already perished from among all nations, when the Gospel is being preached for this purpose, that it may be in all nations?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore the Church remaineth even unto the end of the world, in all nations; and this is the shortness of Her days, because all that is limited is short; so that She may pass into eternity from this brief existence.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: May heretics be lost, may that which they are be lost, and may they be found, that they may be what they are not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Shortness of days will be unto the end of the world: shortness for this reason, because the whole of this season, I say not from this day unto the end of the world, but from Adam down to the end of the world, is a mere drop compared with eternity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not therefore heretics flatter themselves against me, because I said, “the shortness of my days,” as if they would not last down to the end of the world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what hath he added?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O my God, take me not away in the midst of my days” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Deal Thou not with me according as heretics speak.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lead me on unto the end of the world, not only to the middle of my days; and finish my short days, that Thou mayest afterwards grant unto me eternal days.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore then hast thou asked concerning the shortness of thy days?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou wish to hear?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thy years are in the generation of generations.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is why I asked concerning those short days, because although my days should endure unto the end of the world, yet they are short in comparison of Thy days.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “Thy years are in the generation of generations.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore doth he not say, Thy years are unto worlds of worlds; for thus rather is eternity usually signified in the holy Scriptures; but he saith, “Thy years are in the generation of generations”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what are thy years? what, but those which do not come, and then pass away? what, but they which come not, so as to cease again?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For every day in this season so cometh as to cease again; every hour, every month, every year; nothing of these is stationary; before it hath come, it is to be; after it hath come, it will not be.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those everlasting years of thine, therefore, those years that are not changed, “are in the generation of generations.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is a “generation of generations;” in that shall thy years be.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is one such, and if we acknowledge it aright, we shall be in it, and the years of God shall be in us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How shall they be in us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Just as God Himself shall be in us: whence it is said, “That God may be all in all.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the years of God, and God Himself, are not different: but the years of God are the eternity of God: eternity is the very substance of God, which hath nothing changeable; there nothing is past, as if it were no longer: nothing is future, as if it existed not as yet.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is nothing there but, Is: there is not there, Was, and Will be; because what was, is now no longer: and what will be, is not as yet: but whatever is there, simply Is.…Behold this great I Am!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is man’s being to this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To this great I Am, what is man, whatever he be?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who can understand that To Be? who can share it? who can pant, aspire, presume that he may be there?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Despair not, human frailty!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I am,” He saith, “the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast heard what I am in Myself: now hear what I am on thy account.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This eternity then hath called us, and the Word burst forth from eternity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is now eternity, it is now the Word, and no longer time.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 28. …From so many generations thou wilt gather together all the holy offspring of all generations, and wilt form one generation thence: “In” this “generation of generations are Thy years,” that is, that eternity will be in that generation, which is collected from all generations, and reduced into one; this shall share in Thy eternity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Other generations are born for fulfilling their times, out of which this one is regenerated for ever; though changed it shall be endued with life, it shall be fitted to bear Thee, receiving strength from Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou, Lord, in the beginning hast laid the foundation of the earth: and the Heavens are the work of Thy hands” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 25).…God laid the foundation of the earth, we know: the heavens are the works of His hands.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For do not imagine that God doth one thing with His hand, another by His word.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What He doth by His word, He doth by His hand: for He hath not distinct bodily members, who said, “I Am That I Am.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And perhaps His Word is His hand, assuredly His hand is His power.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For inasmuch as it is said, “Let there be a firmament,” and there was a firmament; He is understood to have created it by His Word; but when He said, “Let Us make man in Our image, after Our likeness;” He seemeth to have created him by His hand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear therefore: “The heavens are the work of Thy hands.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lo, what He created by His word, He created also by His hands; because He created them through His excellence, through His power.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Observe rather what He created, and seek not to know in what manner He created them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is much to thee to understand how He created them, since He created thyself so, that thou mayest first be a servant obeying, and afterwards perhaps a friend understanding.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They shall perish, but Thou shalt endure” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Apostle Peter saith this openly: “By the word of God the heavens were of old,” etc. He hath said then that the heavens have already perished by the flood: and we know that the heavens perished as far as the extent of this atmosphere of ours.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the water increased, and filled the whole of that space in which birds fly; thus perished the heavens that are near the earth; those heavens which are meant when we speak of the birds of heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But there are heavens of heavens higher than these in the firmament: but whether these also shall perish by fire, or those only which perished also by the flood, is a much harder question among the learned, nor can it easily, especially in a limited space of time, be explained.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us therefore dismiss or put it off; nevertheless, let us know that these things perish, and that God endureth.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perhaps by the heavens we here may understand, without being far-fetched, the righteous themselves, the saints of God, abiding in whom God hath thundered in His commandments, lightened in His miracles, watered the earth with the wisdom of truth, for “The heavens have declared the glory of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But shall they perish?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Shall they in any sense perish?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In what sense?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As a garment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, as a garment?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As to the body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the body is the garment of the soul; since our Lord called it a garment, when He said, “Is not the life more than meat, and the body than raiment?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How then doth the garment perish?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Though our outward man perish, yet the inward man is renewed day by day.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They then shall perish: but as to the body: “But Thou shalt endure.”…Such heavens therefore shall perish; not, however, for ever; they shall perish, that they may be changed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Doth not the Psalm say this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Read the following: “They shall all wax old as doth a garment; and as a vesture shalt Thou change them, and they shall be changed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hearest of the garment, of the vesture, and dost thou understand anything but the body?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We may therefore hope for the change of our bodies also, but from Him who was before us, and abideth after us.…“But Thou art the same, and Thy years shall not fail” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what are we to those years with these beggarly years? and what are they?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet we ought not to despair.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He had already said in His great and exceeding Wisdom, “I Am That I Am;” and yet He saith to console us, “I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob:” and we are Abraham’s seed: even we, although abject, although dust and ashes, trust in Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We are servants: but for our sakes our Lord took the garb of a servant: for us who are mortal the Immortal One deigned to die, for our sakes He showed His example of resurrection.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us therefore hope that we may reach these lasting years, in which days are not spent in a revolution of the Sun, but what is abideth even as it is, because it alone truly Is.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The children of Thy servants shall dwell there: and their seed shall stand fast for ages” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 28): for the age of ages, the age of eternity, the age that abideth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But, “the children,” he saith, “of Thy servants:” is it to be feared lest we be the servants of God, and our children, and not ourselves, dwell there?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Or if we are the children of the servants, inasmuch as we are the Apostles’ children, what are we to say?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Can those children rising after have so unhappy a presumption, as to boast in their late succession, and so to venture to say, We shall be there; the Apostles will not be there?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: May this be far from their piety as children, from their faith as little ones, from their understanding when of age!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Apostles also will be there: rams go before, lambs follow.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore then, “the children of Thy servants;” and not in brief, “Thy servants”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Both they are Thy servants, and their children are Thy servants; and the children of these, their grandsons, what are they but Thy servants?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou wouldest include them all briefly, if Thou shouldest say, Thy servants shall dwell therein.…“The children of Thy servants,” are the works of Thy servants; no one shall dwell there, but through his own works.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What therefore meaneth, Their children shall dwell?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let no man boast that he shall dwell there, if he calleth himself God’s servant, and hath not works; for none but children shall dwell there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meaneth therefore, “The children of Thy servants shall dwell there”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy servants shall dwell there by their own works, Thy servants shall dwell there through their own children.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be not therefore barren, if thou dost wish to dwell there; send before the children whom thou mayest follow, by sending them before thee, not by burying them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let thy children lead thee to the land of promise, the land of the living, not of the dying: whilst thou art living here in this pilgrimage, let them go before thee, let them receive thee.…
